# Try to download libcoreclr.so debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801

for pattern in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"; do

    echo "  FOUND!"
    DOWNLOAD_URL="$pattern"
    break
  fi
done
echo "---"

  unzip -o -q "$tmpfile" "runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" -d "$tmpdir" 2>/dev/null

toolArgs.command:
# In parallel, try to find the exact version matching the tombstone BuildId
# Download and check BuildIds for versions 10.0.1 through 10.0.5
TARGET_BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

for ver in 10.0.1 10.0.2 10.0.3 10.0.4 10.0.5; do
  echo "Checking $ver..."
  # Download just enough of the nupkg to find libcoreclr.so
  tmpfile="/tmp/rt_${ver}.nupkg"
  curl -sL "https://www.nuget.org/api/v2/package/Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.android-arm64/${ver}" -o "$tmpfile" 2>&1
  # Extract just the libcoreclr.so
  tmpdir="/tmp/rt_${ver}"
  mkdir -p "$tmpdir"
  unzip -o -q "$tmpfile" "runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" -d "$tmpdir" 2>/dev/null
  bid=$(readelf -n "$tmpdir/runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" 2>/dev/null | grep "Build ID" | awk '{print $NF}')
  echo "  BuildId: $bid"
  if [ "$bid" = "$TARGET_BUILDID" ]; then
    echo "  *** MATCH FOUND! Version $ver ***"
  fi
  # Clean up
  rm -f "$tmpfile"
  rm -rf "$tmpdir"
done
